boil: noun. A localized accumulation of pus in the skin, resulting from infection.
broil: verb. To cook by direct, radiant heat.
brr: interjection. Used to express being cold, to show shivering.
hob: noun. A kind of cutting tool, used to cut the teeth of a gear.
holy: adjective. Dedicated to a religious purpose or a god.
horribly: adverb. In a horrible way; very badly.
hoy: noun. A small coaster vessel, usually sloop-rigged, used in conveying passengers and goods, or as a tender to larger vessels in port.
lob: verb. To throw or hit a ball into the air in a high arc.
lorry: noun. A large and heavy motor vehicle designed to carry goods or soldiers; a truck
lory: noun. Any of various small, brightly coloured parrots native to Australasia.
obi: noun. A sash worn with a kimono.
oily: adjective. Covered with or containing oil.
orb: noun. A spherical body; a sphere, especially one of the celestial spheres; a sun, planet, or star
orby: adjective. having characteristics of an orb
rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
rib: noun. Any of a series of long curved bones occurring in 12 pairs in humans and other animals and extending from the spine to or toward the
rob: verb. To steal from, especially using force or violence.
roil: verb. To render turbid by stirring up the dregs or sediment of.
roily: adjective. muddy, cloudy (having lots of sediment)
bhoy: noun. Eye dialect spelling of boy.
bilo: noun. Half a coconut shell, used in Fiji as a cup for drinking alcohol.
biro: noun. A Biro brand ballpoint pen.
blo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anii.
boh: interjection. An exclamation used to startle or frighten.
boryl: noun. Any organic boron radical of the form R 2 B- especially when linked to a metal atom
hoi: interjection. Alternative form of hoy, hey ! a call to gain someone's attention.
horry: adjective. impure; unclean; disgustingly dirty; foul
iyo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mesaka.
lor: particle. Tagged at the end of a sentence to convey a sense of resignation or inevitability.
loy: noun. A type of one-eared spade used in Ireland.
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
yoi: noun. A preparatory position in karate, with the arms slightly forward, elbows slightly bent, and fists closed.
yor: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of your.
